scientific_summary: Shared dedicated VAD segments repaired most of the median transcript disagreement, showing that mismatched ASR boundaries were a major contributor to v1. The remediation still fails participant-coverage and participant-bootstrap lower-bound gates, so population-level measurement stability is not established.

speech_segmentation_and_boundaries.interpretation: Typical timing behavior was stable, but the frozen 0.65 bootstrap-lower requirement failed and not every development participant produced accepted VAD speech.

matched_segment_transcripts.interpretation: Median lexical and semantic model-model agreement passed after boundary matching, but item coverage remained below 0.90 and the frozen character-similarity lower bound remained below 0.40.

boundary_mismatch_attribution.interpretation: Low v1 transcript similarity was largely, but not entirely, caused by comparing transcripts generated on different boundaries.

failure_classification.a_inadequate_german_asr: NOT_THE_DOMINANT_MEDIAN_FAILURE_ON_SUPPORTED_SEGMENTS; population coverage and character lower-bound stability still fail, so the ASR route is not qualified.
failure_classification.b_inadequate_vad_boundary_estimation: PARTIALLY_SUPPORTED; median and perturbation behavior pass, but the participant-bootstrap boundary lower bound fails.
failure_classification.c_insufficient_or_unsuitable_childlens_s: SUPPORTED_AS_A_STOP_REASON; accepted speech was absent for at least one development participant even though the audio track was not mechanically silent and released speech-like timing support existed.
failure_classification.d_inability_to_validate_without_german_h: UNRESOLVED_AND_MATERIAL; model-model stability cannot determine whether unsupported audio contains intelligible German speech or whether either transcript is accurate.

recommendation.bounded_future_option: Obtain authorized German-speaking human review of a separately frozen, privacy-safe development audit before proposing another measurement protocol.
